key_insight: The paper gives exact worst- and best-case distorted utility values under moment ambiguity by reformulating the problem in quantile space and solving it with nonsmooth optimality conditions plus isotonic projection.
engineering_relevance: It provides a plug-in inner oracle for distributionally robust decisions, helping engineers size capacity, set prices, or design contracts while protecting against moment-consistent uncertainty.
method: Recast the extreme-case distorted-utility problem in the quantile domain.
Derive exact first-order optimality conditions for locally Lipschitz, possibly nonsmooth utilities and general distortions.
Use isotonic projection onto the monotone cone to enforce quantile monotonicity efficiently, scaling linearly with discretization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports closed-form extremal values and distributions, and demonstrates the framework on range VaR, GlueVaR with reward-penalty utility, a capped incentive contract under CVaR, and a real generative-AI inference capacity-provisioning case.
watch_item: The approach relies on moment information and a discretized isotonic solve, so performance and fidelity may depend on how well the chosen moments and quantile grid represent the true uncertainty.
paper_title: Extreme-Case Distorted Utility under Moment Ambiguity
